stories.title: The EU-Mercosur deal takes effect — but the fight over it goes on
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Large parts of the EU’s mega trade deal with the Latin American Mercosur bloc finally kicked in Friday, but the political fight over it continues. Negotiations started in 1999 and have been marked by repeated setbacks, leaving officials in Brussels wondering whether the deal to create a 720-million-person free-trade area would ever take effect. The European Commission is provisionally applying the trade part of a broader pact with the Mercosur countries — Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay a

stories.title: As Trump focuses on Iran, Europe and Ukraine strengthen ties
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has largely turned his attention away from the ongoing war in Ukraine — and Europe has stopped waiting for him to refocus. Nearly two months into the Iran war, Trump’s preoccupation with the Middle East has turned Ukraine into an afterthought inside the West Wing, and an organizing principle across the Atlantic. “Things seem to be stuck and need new momentum,” said a European official who, like others interviewed for this report, was granted anonymity to speak candidly. Bu

stories.title: Anger and apathy on the UK campaign trail
stories.summary: SURREY, England — There are impassioned election fights going on in this leafy part of England — but you’d be hard-pressed to find a voter jumping for joy at the prospect. ​Across Surrey’s county and borough councils there’s a united theme: apathy and anger at politicians of all stripes. Rising costs, struggling public services, and general disillusionment with national politics mean even in well-to-do Surrey suburbs — once Conservative strongholds —  the public appears checked o

stories.title: Czech energy group hints at combined bid for British Steel and Speciality Steel UK
stories.summary: Exclusive: Sev.en Global Investments could turn state-owned businesses into Britain’s biggest steelmaker The owner of the UK’s largest electric steelworks has said the government should find a single buyer for British Steel and Speciality Steel UK (SSUK), a move that would create the country’s biggest steelmaker. Sev.en Global Investments, owned by the Czech billionaire Pavel Tykač, said it not only plans to invest £100m in the UK – mainly in the electric arc steelworks in Cardiff it bought last

stories.title: Trump threatens to withdraw US troops from Italy and Spain – as it happened
stories.summary: The US president said he would carry out of a review of US military presence in Europe after public criticism of the US-Israeli war on Iran If you are still planning your summer holidays and looking at some of perhaps more original ways of spending your time crossing Europe, you now have a new option in a train between Prague and Copenhagen via Berlin and Hamburg. The Czech operator, České dráhy, has been somewhat excitedly posting about the latest addition to the growing network of cross-Europe

stories.title: Magyar defends appointing brother-in-law as justice minister
stories.summary: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar defended his decision to pick his brother-in-law as justice minister Friday, calling the lawyer’s professional competence “unquestionable.” Magyar stressed that Márton Melléthei-Barna is the right man for the job and announced in his monologue posted on X that the incoming minister’s wife will make “another sacrifice” and step down as judge “to avoid even the appearance of an intertwining of branches of powe

stories.title: Average price of gas in California edges past $6 a gallon, highest level in four years
stories.summary: Analysts say Americans have now paid $21.7bn more to fill their tanks since the start of the US war on Iran Exxon and Chevron quarterly earnings fall despite soaring oil prices Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The average price for a gallon of gas in California rose to $6 this week as fuel prices across the US reached their highest level in almost four years. The American Automobile Association reported on Friday that California consumers were paying an average of $6.06, while t

stories.title: Hungary: What will become of Orban and his system?
stories.summary: Outgoing Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban and his Fidesz party are still reeling from their election defeat earlier this month. There is much speculation about what their political future looks like.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Az 5x20 millió forintot Wáberer Györgynek hétfőn visszautaljuk
stories.summary: Magyar megnézette, mennyit és mikor utalt át Wáberer György a Tiszának. A döntését meg is indokolta.

stories.title: Magyar Péter húga felfüggeszti bírói tevékenységét
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök hosszan reagált arra, hogy a sógora a Tisza-kormány igazságügyi minisztere lesz, azt mondta, a helyzet neki is komoly dilemmát okozott.

stories.title: Izraeli erők feltartóztattak egy Gázába tartó flottillát, az egyik hajón egy magyar állampolgár is tartózkodott
stories.summary: Gázába vittek volna segélyt, a hajók most Kréta partjainál vannak, a magyar külügy szerint konzuljuk hamarosan a helyszínre érkezik.

stories.title: Csak egy uniós országban készíti ki fizikailag jobban a munka a dolgozókat, mint itthon
stories.summary: Az EU más országaiban dolgozókhoz képest nagy arányban számolnak be fizikai és mentális túlterheltségről a magyarok, ez pedig gyengébb teljesítményhez és magasabb fluktuációhoz vezethet.

stories.title: Trump bejelentette, hogy jövő héten megemeli az európai autókra kivetett vámot
stories.summary: 25 százalék vonatkozik majd többek között a Magyarországon gyártott autókra is.

stories.title: Kétharmados csalódás és szelfipont a sztárrá avanzsált kilakoltatós műsornál: így telt a Mi Hazánk majálisa
stories.summary: Sok Mi Hazánk-szavazó örül, hogy megbukott a Fidesz, de annak nem, hogy a Tisza mért rájuk ekkora vereséget. Magyar Péter személye Toroczkai László beszédének is központi eleme volt, de még talán ez sem érdekelte annyira az embereket, mint a Bűnvadászok fekete furgonja.

stories.title: Magyar Orvosi Kamara: Tüneti kezelés lenne a szabad orvosválasztás visszavezetése
stories.summary: A kamara Kapitány István leendő gazdasági miniszter szabad szülésorvos-választásról szóló bejegyzésére reagált.

stories.title: Bod Péter Ákos az euróbevezetésről: Nincsenek komoly érvek, hogy megtartsuk a forintot
stories.summary: Az euró bevezetéséhez a magyar költségvetést rendbe kell tenni, és itt kezdődnek a gondok. Mik a hosszú távú előnyök, és mit fogunk érezni közvetlenül azután, ha átálltunk euróra? A Téma vendége Bod Péter Ákos közgazdász, egyetemi tanár, az MTA doktora.

stories.title: Die unterschätzte Rolle Chinas im Iran-Krieg – mit Felix Lee
stories.summary: Während die USA und der Iran in einen militärischen Konflikt am Golf verwickelt sind, scheint China sich vornehm zurückzuhalten. Aber der Schein trügt. Rixa Fürsen spricht mit dem China-Spezialisten Felix Lee über Pekings Doppelstrategie. Warum blockiert China Resolutionen im UN-Sicherheitsrat, obwohl es massiv auf Ölimporte aus der Golfregion angewiesen ist? Lee analysiert Chinas „Energiewende“, die vor allem sicherheitspolitisch motiviert ist und weshalb das Land durch seine massiven Reserven

stories.title: The missing Ukrainian reporter, the Russian prison – and a vital lesson learned about journalism in a dangerous age | Laurent Richard
stories.summary: To do our job, investigative journalists are learning to work across borders. This isn’t just about the truth, it’s about staying alive Laurent Richard is a journalist and the director of the Forbidden Stories consortium In February 2025, the body of journalist Viktoriia Roshchyna was finally returned to Ukraine after months of uncertainty. She was one of 757 Ukrainian casualties handed over by Russian authorities as part of an exchange of prisoners and the dead. Roshchyna had disappeared in the

stories.title: Leszerelt a honvédségtől az egyik legmagasabb szintre jutó magyar katona
stories.summary: Porkoláb Imre korábban a Pentagonban is dolgozott, az elmúlt években pedig Orbán Viktor nemzetbiztonsági főtanácsadójának munkatársa volt.

stories.title: Németh Szilárdot beidézte a Fővárosi Törvényszék
stories.summary: A leköszönő kormánypárt rezsibiztosának a hozzá köthető Kozma István Magyar Birkózó Akadémia ellen indított 534 milliós kártérítési per miatt kellett megjelennie.

stories.title: Közleményben üzent a leendő igazságügyi miniszternek a Magyar Bírói Egyesület
stories.summary: Azt szeretnék, hogy az új kormány érdemben vonja be őket az igazságszolgáltatást érintő ügyekbe.

stories.title: Sokáig gond nélkül gyűjthették a befektetők pénzét az árnyékbankok, de most megindult a lavina
stories.summary: Miután az ügyfelek tömegesen kezdtek el pénzt kivonni a hitelalapokból, neves cégek is korlátozni kezdték ennek lehetőségét. Nem kellett sokat várni arra sem, hogy előkerüljön a 2008-as pénzügyi válsággal való esetleges párhuzam. Mekkora a baj valójában?

stories.title: Ha valahol van diktatúra az országban, akkor az a Fidesz – a fideszes képviselő, akit kiborított Pócs János
stories.summary: A sírás kerülgette, annyira kiakadt Pócs János parlamentbe jutásán. A Telexnek most azt mondta, csapdába került a Fidesz. Orbánnal nincs megújulás, Orbán nélkül viszont nincs Fidesz.

stories.title: A Szajnába zuhant egy busz Párizs mellett, búvárok mentettek ki négy embert a vízből
stories.summary: A baleset okát még vizsgálják.

stories.title: A CIA bedrótozott kémmacskákat akart bevetni a hidegháborúban
stories.summary: Egyvalamivel nem számoltak: hogy a macska híresen engedetlen állat, és nem oda megy, ahova küldik, hanem oda, ahova akar. 20 millió dollárt költhettek a projektre, de eredménye nem lett.

stories.title: Avalanche deaths have surged in the Alps this season. Here’s why | Johan Gaume
stories.summary: As an ex-pro snowboarder, I know the temptations of fresh snowfall. And as an avalanche researcher, I know how easily people can get into trouble Many avalanche accidents occur just after a storm, when blue-sky conditions return and people head to the backcountry to enjoy fresh powder. These kinds of accidents happen every year, despite warnings to those heading out into the snow. It is easy to dismiss some behaviour as reckless, and sometimes it is – but it is also human behaviour, and it is no

stories.title: Hungary's Magyar meets EU's von der Leyen in high-stakes Brussels visit
stories.summary: Even before Peter Magyar takes office as Hungary's prime minister, he is looking to unlock €10 billion in EU funding ahead of an August deadline. Talks with Ursula von der Leyen come as part of a post-Orban EU reset.

stories.title: EU trade official calls Trump tariff threat ‘unacceptable’ after president says trucks and cars will face 25% levy – US politics live
stories.summary: EU official responds after US president accuses EU of non-compliance Trump tears up EU tariff deal and raises some import duties Sign up for the Breaking News US email Meanwhile, the White House has said it will not detail private diplomatic conversations when Reuters asked about Iran ’s new proposal to the United States that was submitted to Pakistani mediators. “We do not detail private diplomatic conversations. President Trump has been clear that Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon , and

stories.title: Turkish police fire tear gas and arrest at least 570 people at May Day rallies
stories.summary: Earlier this week, authorities issued arrest warrants for 62 people, of whom they deemed 46, including journalists, trade unionists and opposition figures, were "likely to carry out attacks."

stories.title: Trump threatens to raise tariffs on EU automobiles to 25 percent
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Friday said he will raise tariffs on automobiles and auto parts from the European Union because the 27-country bloc is not living up to its end of a trade agreement struck last summer. “I am pleased to announce that, based on the fact the European Union is not complying with our fully agreed to Trade Deal, next week I will be increasing Tariffs charged to the European Union for Cars and Trucks coming into the United States,” Trump wrote on Truth Social. “The Tariff will

stories.title: Trump threatens to withdraw troops from Italy and Spain
stories.summary: US president says European countries are ‘absolutely horrible’ to refuse to support operations in strait of Hormuz Donald Trump has threatened to withdraw US troops from Italy and Spain, a day after saying he was looking at reducing the number deployed in Germany. The US president’s threat to Germany came after its chancellor, Friedrich Merz, said America was being “humiliated” by Iran, and follows weeks of criticism by Trump of Nato allies for not helping to reopen the strait of Hormuz. Continu

stories.title: Florida prosecutors launch criminal investigation into deaths of 31 sloths
stories.summary: Sloths were set to be displayed at controversial new theme park but report revealed mammals died in warehouse Prosecutors in Florida said Friday they have launched a criminal investigation into the deaths of dozens of sloths from South America that were set to be displayed at a controversial new theme park. A Florida fish and wildlife commission (FWC) report revealed last week that 31 mammals taken from rainforests in Peru and Guyana by the owners of Sloth World, a forthcoming tourist attraction

stories.title: Trump tears up part of EU tariff deal and raises some import duties
stories.summary: US president says tariff on cars and lorries will rise to 25% and accuses European Union of non-compliance Donald Trump has said he is tearing up part of the tariff deal he struck with EU leaders at his golf course in Scotland last summer, criticising Brussels for taking so long to ratify the deal. Blindsiding Brussels late on Friday, a public holiday in much of Europe, he announced that he would be increasing tariffs on cars and lorries imported into the US from the EU from 15% to 25% from next

stories.title: May Day marked by protests, celebrations, arrests
stories.summary: Labor Day comes as rising energy costs and shrinking purchasing triggered by the Iran war has hurt workers around the world.

stories.title: Attempted murder charge after Jewish men stabbed in London
stories.summary: LONDON — A 45-year-old man has been charged with attempted murder after two Jewish men were stabbed Wednesday in the U.K. capital. Essa Suleiman faces two counts of attempted murder and one count of possessing a bladed article in a public place following the stabbing of two Jewish men — Shloime Rand, aged 34, and Moshe Shine, aged 76. The attack took place in Golders Green, north west London. Both were taken to hospital, with Rand subsequently discharged. Suleiman was also charged with attempted

stories.title: Trump threatens Sánchez and Meloni with US troops withdrawal
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday he would “probably” remove American troops from Spain and Italy, in renewed attacks against Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez and Italian leader Giorgia Meloni. Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office , the American leader said Italy had “not been of any help to us,” and accused Spain of being “absolutely horrible.” Both Spain and Italy have denied U.S. military planes that are taking part in the Iran war from usin

stories.title: Antigua and Barbuda prime minister set to win fourth term in election
stories.summary: Gaston Browne is on course to win 15 of the 17 seats in parliament after calling snap election Gaston Browne, the prime minister of Antigua and Barbuda, is set to win a fourth term in the country’s snap general election with preliminary results showing his party on course to win 15 of the 17 seats in parliament. Addressing supporters early on Friday morning, Browne said: “You have spoken, you have spoken clearly. You have indicated that the Antigua and Barbuda Labour party (ABLP) is the best ins

stories.title: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?
stories.summary: Colombia hosted nearly 60 countries at pivotal time on world stage for fight to transition to a clean energy future Looking out to sea from the grey sandy beaches of Santa Marta, on Colombia’s Caribbean coast, it is never hard to spot evidence of the country’s thriving fossil fuel export trade. Oil tankers ride at anchor on the horizon and sometimes, locals say, lumps of coal wash up on the shore, blown off the collier ships that carry cargos from the nearby mines. It was here, on Wednesday even

stories.title: Cuban immigrant dies in Georgia detention center, ICE tells Congress
stories.summary: Denny Adán González, 33, whose death is being investigated as suicide, is 18th person to die in ICE custody this year A Cuban immigrant died inside an immigration detention center in Georgia earlier this week, according to a congressional notification sent on Friday and reviewed by the Guardian. The Cuban man, identified as 33-year-old Denny Adán González, died inside the privately run Stewart detention center. His death is being investigated as a suicide. Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( I

stories.title: UK defence firm Ultra Electronics to pay £15m after SFO bribery investigation
stories.summary: Company accepts it failed to prevent bribery in connection with contracts in Algeria and Oman sought through agents The British defence company Ultra Electronics has accepted responsibility for a failure to prevent bribery and agreed to pay £15m after an investigation by the Serious Fraud Office . The penalties are part of a deferred prosecution approved by the high court on Friday, after an investigation opened in 2018 when the company referred itself to the UK law enforcement agency a month af

stories.title: Starmer says Polanski ‘is not fit to lead a political party’ after Golders Green police criticism
stories.summary: Green leader apologises for sharing post that said officers were ‘repeatedly and violently kicking a mentally ill man in the head’ and says he had did so ‘in haste’ Keir Starmer has condemned Zack Polanski as “disgraceful” and unfit to head a political party after the Greens’ leader shared a social media post critical of the way police tackled the suspect in the Golders Green stabbings. The prime minister said any criticism of the police involved in the arrest was unfair on officers having to ma

stories.title: Brazil’s congress approves bill reducing prison sentence of former president Jair Bolsonaro
stories.summary: President Lula’s veto of the bill was overturned by Brazil’s congress and senate, meaning it now awaits confirmation by supreme court Brazil’s largely conservative congress has approved a bill reducing the prison sentence of the far-right former president Jair Bolsonaro, who was convicted last year of attempting a coup. The bill had initially been passed by congress in December, but President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva vetoed it in January in a symbolic move marking three years since Bolsonaro su

stories.title: Myanmar: Ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest
stories.summary: Aung San Suu Kyi's son said the transfer did not mean freedom and called for the military junta to provide "proof of life." The former leader has not been seen in public since 2021.

stories.title: Germany news: Greens and CDU agree Baden-Württemberg deal
stories.summary: The state of Baden-Württemberg's Greens and conservatives have forged a coalition deal after weeks of talks. Meanwhile, unions are staging May Day rallies to demand better work conditions and a wealth tax. DW has more.

stories.title: Pentagon inks deals with AI giants, but not Anthropic
stories.summary: The pacts enable OpenAI, Google and SpaceX to bring top-tier artificial intelligence (AI) models onto the US military’s secure classified networks.

stories.title: Trump to raise tariffs on cars, trucks from EU to 25%
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he's raising tariffs on cars and trucks from the European Union to 25%, saying the bloc was not complying with the terms of the trade agreement agreed to with the US.

stories.title: Ukraine plans army pay hikes, phased discharge — Zelenskyy
stories.summary: The Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced reforms to boost morale and address military complaints about exhaustion and uneven workloads on the frontline.

stories.title: US imposes sanctions on DR Congo ex-President Kabila alleging rebel support
stories.summary: The former leader has not responded to US accusations that he is aiming to destabilise the government.

stories.title: Iran executes 21-year-old karate champion as UN warns of death penalty surge
stories.summary: Tehran executed 21-year-old karate athlete Sassan Azadvar early Thursday after protest arrest, as UN and rights groups warn of a surge in executions and mass detentions since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Turkish police arrest more than 500 people at May Day rallies
stories.summary: Turkey sees significant police deployments on 1 May each year, when marches led by workers and unions are held.

stories.title: Iran war impact on shipping affecting refugee aid with freight rates soaring, UNHCR says
stories.summary: The agency has re-routed sea cargo via Jordan's Aqaba Red Sea port and has switched to land corridors including truck routes from Dubai across the Arabian peninsula and Turkey.

stories.title: Trump bets on quick Iran oil crunch. Experts see prolonged pain and rising costs.
stories.summary: The Trump administration insists that its naval blockade is putting the squeeze on Iran and that the regime is just a “matter of days” away from an energy crisis unless it capitulates. Energy experts are skeptical and say the White House is misreading both the timing of the harm to the Iranian oil industry as well as the regime’s tolerance for pain. The standoff comes as benchmark oil prices hit a four-year high Thursday while the average price at the pump  jumped to $4.30 a gallon, up 27 c

stories.title: Weather tracker: Tornadoes and giant hail in central US
stories.summary: Strongest tornado hits Mineral Wells, Texas, where disaster was declared. Elsewhere, extreme rain inundates China Spring is the season for severe thunderstorms across the central US, and the start of this week was a particularly active period for the region. A favourable weather pattern fuelled intense thunderstorms on Monday through Wednesday, bringing strong winds, very large hail and strong tornadoes. Eight tornadoes were reported on Monday, including an EF2 tornado that ripped through the to

stories.title: ‘Barely a street’ in Dubbo without trees poisoned by pesticides, NSW Greens say – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A man has allegedly stolen nearly $3,000 worth of diesel in jerry cans and drums from a South Sydney service station. Police said they were told a driver of a white ute allegedly filled large drums and jerry cans with 915 litres of diesel, valued at over $2,870 (or about $3.14 a litre), without paying at about 7.15pm on 11 April. Continue reading...

stories.title: Uganda copying Russia and China with new bill designed to crush dissent, say critics
stories.summary: New law proposes up to 20 years in prison for promoting ‘foreign interests’, and restricts those who work with or are funded by overseas partners Ugandan opposition figures, human rights organisations and legal experts have condemned a sweeping bill that proposes up to 20 years in prison for promoting “foreign interests”, and imposes restrictions on a broad range of people and organisations that work with or receive funding from overseas partners. The protection of sovereignty bill 2026 is being

stories.title: Italian city orders dog owners to wash away urine or face €500 fine
stories.summary: Livorno council says residents have complained of foul smell after rise in number of pets Dog owners in an Italian port city will be required to clean up their pets’ urine from public spaces or face fines of up to €500. Luca Salvetti, the mayor of Livorno, on the Tuscan coast, introduced the measure after complaints from residents about the smell of dog urine, particularly in parks and children’s play areas. Continue reading...

stories.title: Octopus Energy boss: some people would accept blackouts if bills cut
stories.summary: Greg Jackson argues against costly investments in UK’s power grid that are adding to household bills The boss of the UK’s biggest energy supplier has suggested that some households would accept an occasional electricity blackout in exchange for much lower energy bills. A year on from Europe’s largest power outage – which left tens of millions of people in Spain and Portugal without trains, metros, traffic lights, ATMs, phone connections and internet access – the chief executive of Octopus Energy

stories.title: CCTV captures moment man attacks nun in Jerusalem – video
stories.summary: Israeli police released a video on Thursday showing an attack on a French Catholic nun in Jerusalem. The footage shows the man running up behind the nun and pushing her to the ground. After briefly walking away, the attacker, who appears to be Jewish, returns to kick the nun as she lies on the ground and only stops when a passerby intervenes. Police said they had arrested a 36-year-old man. The date when the video was recorded was verified by an official statement from the Israeli foreign minist

stories.title: Video of attack on French nun in Jerusalem causes widespread condemnation
stories.summary: Israeli foreign ministry denounces ‘shameful act’ after footage shows man pushing woman to ground and kicking her A video of an attack on a French Catholic nun and archeological researcher in Jerusalem has caused widespread revulsion and been denounced as a “shameful act” by Israel’s foreign ministry. In the video, a man runs up behind the nun as she walks down a street and pushes her over with force, so that the victim comes close to hitting her head on a block of stone. After walking away a fe

stories.title: Woman charged over death of two eight-year-old girls after Wimbledon car crash
stories.summary: Claire Freemantle accused of causing death and serious injury by dangerous driving when vehicle hit school in 2023 The driver of a car that crashed into a south London primary school has been charged with causing death by dangerous driving after two eight-year-old girls were killed. Claire Freemantle is accused of two counts of causing death by dangerous driving and seven counts of causing serious injury by dangerous driving after the incident at The Study Prep school in Wimbledon in July 2023.

stories.title: Sabastian Sawe receives hero’s welcome in Kenya after sub-two hour marathon feat
stories.summary: Record-breaker says London Marathon win was ‘a victory for all of us’ as he is greeted by family and friends in Eldoret Hugged, cheered and adorned with garlands, the first man to run an official marathon in under two hours has returned as a hero to his home village in Kenya. Sabastian Sawe, who stunned the world when he clocked 1h 59m 30s in the London Marathon last weekend, flew in a Kenyan military plane normally reserved for special operations on Thursday to his home region of western Kenya.

stories.title: Iran offers new peace proposal to US but Trump ‘not satisfied’
stories.summary: Tehran reportedly passed proposal to mediators in Pakistan on Thursday night, though its contents are not yet clear Iran has passed a new proposal to Pakistani mediators in the latest effort to end the war with the US, but Donald Trump said he was not “satisfied” by it. “Right now, we have talks going on, they’re not getting there,” he told reporters, adding that his options remained “either blast them away or make a deal”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Andy Burnham has plan to return to Westminster ‘within weeks’, allies say
stories.summary: Exclusive: Greater Manchester mayor said to have identified seats where MPs would step aside to allow leadership bid ‘No other plan comes close’: Labour MPs turn to Burnham as pressure mounts on Starmer Andy Burnham has a credible plan to return to Westminster “within weeks”, his allies have said, with the Greater Manchester mayor expected to use a byelection fight to set out a new agenda for government. Burnham, who was blocked by Labour’s ruling body from running in February’s Gorton and Dento

stories.title: ‘No other plan comes close’: how Labour MPs turned to Burnham with Starmer on the brink
stories.summary: Greater Manchester mayor’s team have been quietly preparing a manifesto and identifying seats where MPs could step aside to allow a Westminster run Andy Burnham has plan to return to Westminster ‘within weeks’, allies say When the eyes of Westminster were on the committee rooms and voting lobbies of parliament this week, Keir Starmer’s political future was being decided elsewhere. Wes Streeting and Angela Rayner were buttering up Labour MPs in the Strangers’ Bar in parliament as colleagues spoke

stories.title: Small plane crash in Texas Hill Country leaves five dead
stories.summary: All people onboard Cessna 421C dead after crash late at night in city 40 miles south-west of state capital Austin A small plane crashed among trees in Texas Hill Country, killing all five people onboard, officials said on Friday. The crash happened in the dark late on Thursday night in Wimberley, a city about 40 miles south-west of the state capital, Austin, the Hays county judge, Ruben Becerra, said in a post on Facebook. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Profoundly distressing’: record number of deaths in custody and police operations in NSW
stories.summary: There were 66 deaths in custody and police operations in 2025 – 18 more than the previous year Warning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have died There were a record number of New South Wales deaths in custody and police operations last year, with almost a quarter of prisoner deaths recorded as self-harm, a coroner’s court report states. Of those who killed themselves, most were by hanging, despite millions being invested to remove ligature points . Continue readin

stories.title: India news: Commercial LPG prices hiked, new cylinder rules take effect
stories.summary: India has increased the prices of LPG used by the restraunt and hotel industry. It is the third such monthly hike since the Iran war upended global suppply.

stories.title: UK: Golders Green suspect charged in stabbings of Jewish men
stories.summary: The Golders Green stabbings are the latest in a string of attacks on London's Jewish community in recent weeks.

stories.title: Iran war expands press crackdown, leaving information vacuum
stories.summary: For many journalists in Iran, the truth is increasingly being treated as a security threat. Authorities are tightening an already repressive reporting environment, where information is strictly controlled.

stories.title: Is Myanmar's military winning the civil war?
stories.summary: After five years of war, armed resistance groups still challenge Myanmar's military across much of the country. But the generals are now stemming losses and reclaiming some territory.

stories.title: Germany needs no tips from Donald Trump, says Merz's deputy
stories.summary: Germany's vice chancellor says US President Donald Trump has made a mess of the Iran war and that his focus should be on ending it. As fuel prices soar, Lars Klingbeil said Trump bears responsibility for the fallout.

stories.title: Germany: Lufthansa apologizes for lost Oscar statuette
stories.summary: Lusthansa said it would conduct "an internal review" to find out how the Oscar statuette belonging to Russian director Pavel Talankin went missing in Frankfurt.

stories.title: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.summary: Seventeen people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

stories.title: Vonn still in 'survival mode' after Olympic crash
stories.summary: American skiing great Lindsey Vonn says she is still "too emotional" to make a decision on her future career as she faces at least 18 months of recuperation.

stories.title: Israel releases all but two activists in Greece after intercepting Gaza aid flotilla
stories.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla denounced the interception as "piracy", while Israel's foreign ministry called the flotilla a "PR stunt".

stories.title: Japanese man arrested after wife's remains found in zoo incinerator, officials say
stories.summary: The victim once told her relatives that her husband was threatening to "burn you until no trace of you will be left," public broadcaster NHK said, citing investigative sources.

stories.title: King Charles III wraps up US state visit with Arlington tribute
stories.summary: King Charles III concluded his four-day US state visit with appearances in Virginia, a tribute at Arlington National Cemetery and a farewell ceremony before departing from Maryland.

stories.title: Missing Oscar found after Academy Award winner blocked from taking it on flight
stories.summary: The airline said the Academy Award was safely in its care in Frankfurt and was arranging its return.

stories.title: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
stories.summary: A shortage of fertiliser due to the Iran conflict could reduce crop yields and push prices higher, says the boss of Yara.

stories.title: Kiwi birds return to New Zealand's capital after a century away
stories.summary: Wellington has welcomed its 250th relocated kiwi as the Capital Kiwi Project works to restore New Zealand's national bird to hills where it has been absent for over a century.

stories.title: Israel arrests man filmed attacking Catholic nun near Jerusalem’s Old City
stories.summary: Israeli police arrested a 36-year-old man who was filmed assaulting a French Catholic nun near Jerusalem’s Old City, in what it said was "zero tolerance to all acts of violence".

stories.title: Brixen Water Light Festival 2026 puts peace at its centre
stories.summary: The Brixen Water Light Festival 2026 runs to 16 May in South Tyrol, with 24 installations from 13 countries exploring peace against a Dolomites backdrop.

stories.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
stories.summary: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

stories.title: Israeli police arrest man after nun attacked in Jerusalem
stories.summary: The French nun was pushed over and kicked in the apparently unprovoked assault, amid a rise in harassment of Christians in the city by Jewish extremists.

stories.title: Zayn Malik cancels US tour and cuts UK dates after hospital bed photo shared
stories.summary: The former One Direction singer has taken to Instagram to update fans as he cuts his world tour dates

stories.title: French PM fuels row with trip to buy baguettes
stories.summary: Sébastien Lecornu visited a boulangerie on Labour Day, defying unions who say 1 May must remain a mandatory rest day.

stories.title: Russia launches new Soyuz-5 rocket from Kazakhstan cosmodrome in first test flight
stories.summary: On Thursday, April 30, at 23:00 local time (Astana), the Baikonur Cosmodrome witnessed the first test launch of the Soyuz-5/Sunkar vehicle. The rocket lifted off from Site 45 as part of flight development tests, according to Kazakhstan’s Ministry of AI and Digital Development.

stories.title: Trump repeats that he is 'not happy' with Iran as deadline to seek approval for war looms
stories.summary: President Trump said on Friday that said a deal has been hard to reach in part because Iranian leadership was "very confused".

stories.title: US President Donald Trump says 'not satisfied' with new peace proposal from Iran
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Washington is gripped by a legalistic debate over whether Trump has passed a deadline for requesting congressional approval for the war.

stories.title: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

stories.title: Trump signs bill to end record shutdown over immigration enforcement
stories.summary: The shutdown caused chaos in airports across the US as politicians feuded over funds for Trump's immigration crackdown.

stories.title: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Russia’s Tuapse after Ukrainian drone strikes hit oil refinery
stories.summary: Plumes of black smoke rise over the southern Russian town of Tuapse after multiple Ukrainian drone strikes on a major oil refinery in the coastal area.

stories.title: Australian hiker missing in Nova Scotia national park not heard from for two weeks
stories.summary: Denise Ann Williams, 62, was last heard from on 15 April, when she told her family she was travelling to the west coast of Cape Breton Island in Canada’s east A search is underway in Canada for a 62-year-old Australian woman who was reported missing on Tuesday while hiking in a coastal national park in the country’s south-east. Denise Ann Williams was last heard from on 15 April, when she told family she was travelling to Chéticamp, a fishing village on the west coast of Cape Breton Island in th

stories.title: Trump administration says hostilities in Iran ‘terminated’ ahead of war powers deadline
stories.summary: US president faced a 60-day deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending it A US-Iran ceasefire that began in early April has “terminated” hostilities between the two sides for the purposes of an approaching congressional war powers deadline, a senior official of the Trump administration said on Thursday. Donald Trump faced a deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending it, but the date was most likely to pass without alte

stories.title: Police say they will charge people with unrest in Alice Springs after arrest over death of Kumanjayi Little Baby
stories.summary: Five-year-old’s grandfather and senior Warlpiri elder appeals for calm, saying: ‘It is time now for sorry business’ • Warning: This article contains references to and images of Indigenous Australians who have died Northern Territory police say one person is facing charges and more are expected to be over unrest in Alice Springs after the arrest of a man in connection with the death of five-year-old girl. The grandfather of Kumanjayi Little Baby, whose body was found on Thursday 5km from the Old

stories.title: Iran war may cause food shortages in Africa, world’s largest fertiliser firm says
stories.summary: Yara CEO warns of global auction that would leave poorest countries scrambling for supplies they can ill afford The Iran war could have “dramatic consequences”, causing food shortages and price rises in some of Africa’s poorest and most vulnerable communities, the head of the world’s largest fertiliser company has said. Svein Tore Holsether, the chief executive of Yara International, said world leaders needed to guard against soaring prices and shortages of fertiliser causing a de facto global a

stories.title: Germany’s climate U-turn is the worst possible response to the oil shock
stories.summary: Prices at the pump have leapt since the start of the conflict – but clinging to fossil fuels will only prolong the pain The car is perhaps the closest thing Germany has to a national symbol. For this reason, the success of the auto industry and the happiness of motorists has long been a barometer for the standing of the Federal Republic. Since the beginning of the war on Iran, German news has been filled with stories about drivers. Journalists have filed breathless dispatches from petrol station

stories.title: BAE faces £120m lawsuit over decision to scrap support for aid aircraft
stories.summary: EnComm Aviation says the firm’s action has cut off vital support for crisis-hit countries including South Sudan and the DRC Britain’s biggest weapons manufacturer, BAE Systems, is facing a £120m lawsuit after scrapping support for aircraft used to deliver aid to some of the world’s neediest countries. EnComm Aviation, a Kenya-based aid cargo operator, claims the decision forced the cancellation of humanitarian contracts and reduced supplies to South Sudan, now threatened by famine , Somalia and

stories.title: US prosecutors release new footage of Trump shooting suspect
stories.summary: The Justice Department has released unseen footage in an attempt to show that the secret service agent injured in the attack was not shot by friendly fire.

stories.title: First direct US-Venezuela flight in 7 years lands in Caracas
stories.summary: The direct flight comes a month after the US formally reopened its embassy in Caracas following the kidnapping of President Nicolas Maduro in January.

stories.title: Gender investment gap: Are women catching up with men?
stories.summary: Online brokers are tapping into the potential of female investors. Tailor-made products and branding campaigns are on the rise. Will this help bridge the gap between women and men when it comes to investing?

stories.title: Trump lifts tariffs on Scotch whisky in nod to King Charles
stories.summary: Trump said he was lifting the tariffs on whiskey from the UK in honor of the British King and Queen, announcing the move the day the royal duo departed from the US.

stories.title: Iran delivers fresh talks proposal to Pakistani mediators
stories.summary: Iran has delivered a new proposal for negotiations with the US to Pakistani mediators, state news agency IRNA said. Iranian foreign minister also spoke with several of his counterparts, including EU's Kaja Kallas.

stories.title: Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, as spat with Merz intensifies
stories.summary: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

stories.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.title: Britney Spears charged in California with driving under influence
stories.summary: The pop star is facing one misdemeanour count of driving under the influence of any alcohol and drug.

stories.title: New CCTV footage appears to shows when shots were fired at Washington press dinner
stories.summary: The video was released after claims that a secret service member sustained injuries in an act of 'friendly fire'.

stories.title: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition
stories.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.title: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.title: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
stories.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

stories.title: Violence in Australian town after arrest of man over girl's murder
stories.summary: Unrest took place outside hospital where man suspected of murdering a five-year-old girl was being treated.

stories.title: Former Chick-fil-A employee charged in $80,000 mac-and-cheese scheme
stories.summary: The Texas man allegedly refunded the orders of hundreds of catering-sized mac-and-cheese trays to his personal credit cards.

stories.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
stories.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

stories.title: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

stories.title: Trump mulls US troop cuts in Italy and Spain over Iran war row
stories.summary: Trump suggested his administration was also looking into reducing its presence in Spain and Italy over clashes with their leaders on the war in Iran. The comments come just one day after the US president made a similar proposal in Germany.

stories.title: Crowds gather in central London after Banksy confirms new statue
stories.summary: Crowds gathered in central London after Banksy confirmed he was behind a newly installed statue in Waterloo Place.

stories.title: Six injured in Washington state school stabbing
stories.summary: Five students and one security guard were taken to hospital following the attack at Foss High School.

stories.title: Brazil's Congress reduces ex-President Bolsonaro's coup prison sentence
stories.summary: Lawmakers defied President Lula da Silva’s veto and moved to adopt a bill that would dramatically reduce former President Jair Bolsonaro’s 27-year prison sentence for plotting a coup following his loss in the October 2022 presidential elections.

stories.title: Fölösleges a dízel, Európa legnépszerűbb Volkswagenje benzinmotorral is takarékos
stories.summary: A T-Rocból tavaly több mint 200 ezer példányt adtak el Európában, ezért kíváncsiak voltunk a népszerűség okaira, illetve arra, hogy érdemes-e extrákkal teleszórt verziót venni belőle.

stories.summary: A vállalkozók bíznak abban, hogy most már inkább a szakmaiságra terelődik a figyelem, és másodlagos lesz, hogy kinek milyen politikai véleménye van.

stories.summary: Egy kis átmozgatás, és már meg is van a matt.

stories.summary: A cég azt írta, megteszik és megtették a szükséges jogi lépéseket.

stories.summary: Azért lépett ki az MTA korábbi elnöke, „mert a HUN-REN felsővezetői az utóbbi időszakban számos olyan kijelentést tettek, amelyekkel nem tudok azonosulni”.

stories.title: Hollywoodi film készül Örkény Tóték című kisregényéből, a Fehér Lótusz sztárja lesz az egyik főszereplő
stories.summary: Peter Fellows rendezi, ez lesz az első nagyjátékfilmje.

stories.title: Ha a klímaváltozás élesbe kapcsol, a Margit-szigetet elfelejthetjük árvíz idején
stories.summary: A klímamodellek szerint nem a csapadék mennyisége, hanem az intenzitása fog megváltozni a jövőben. Az eddigi évi 20-30 esőzés helyett 4-5 özönvízből jön majd össze az évi 600 mm csapadék, ami után hosszabb, száraz időszakok jönnek. De hogyan lehet ehhez alkalmazkodni?

stories.summary: A leendő gazdasági és energetikai miniszter azt is írta, hogy biztosítják az emberséges szülészeti ellátásokat és hatékony meddőségi kezeléseket vezetnek be.

stories.summary: Egy budai mélygarázsban le is fotózták az autókat.

stories.summary: Megszereztük azt a beszámolót, amely a Márai- és a Jókai-emlékév 130 milliós támogatásáról szólt, és amelyet az NKA Bizottsága valójában elfogadott mint „kiemelkedő jelentőségű” pályázatokat. Miután nyilvánossá vált a valós, 17 milliárdos lista, még a bizottság egyik meghatározó tagjának, Vidnyánszky Attilának is leesett az álla, hogy „milyen pénzek mentek és kiknek”. A testületből többen lemondtak, mások vizsgálatot kezdeményeztek.

stories.title: How Serbia's government dominates the country's media
stories.summary: Since Serbia's ruling SNS party came to power, it has tightened its grip on the media. With an election expected soon, experts fear that authorities will try to eliminate the last pockets of independent reporting.

stories.title: US: Trump signs DHS funding bill, ending partial shutdown
stories.summary: The legislation ends a partial government shutdown and restores funding to key government agencies. But Republicans are unhappy that ICE and Border Patrol have been excluded.

stories.title: US seeks 'maritime freedom' coalition to restart Strait of Hormuz shipping
stories.summary: Iran effectively closed the crucial waterway when the war broke out on 28 February. Normally one-fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas transits the waterway. Its closure has sent energy prices skyrocketing around the world.

stories.summary: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

stories.summary: Patrick Vallance says government working with Chinese officials to remove postings from Alibaba after Biobank data breach last week There have been further listings of confidential health records of UK volunteers on the Chinese website Alibaba since the breach reported last week, and the government is braced for further leaks, the science minister has said. Addressing a House of Lords debate on the attempted sale of data belonging to 500,000 UK Biobank volunteers, Patrick Vallance said the gover

stories.title: South Africa deports Mugabe’s son for unrelated offences after employee shot at family home
stories.summary: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe also fined after pleading guilty to immigration and firearms-related offences Two months after an employee was shot in the back at the Mugabe family home in a wealthy suburb of Johannesburg, a South African court has fined and ordered the deportation of Robert Mugabe’s youngest son over two unrelated charges. Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, 28, and his cousin Tobias Mugabe Matonhodze, 33, were initially both charged with attempted murder after the incident on 19 February.

stories.title: At least five killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza including a nine-year-old boy
stories.summary: Eighty-one sick and injured children accompanied by their families were evacuated from Gaza to receive medical treatment in Jordan. They travelled via the King Hussein Bridge to various government and private hospitals in the capital Amman.

stories.title: Christchurch gunman fails in bid to appeal against guilty pleas in New Zealand court
stories.summary: Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslims said poor mental health made him admit to crimes The Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslim worshippers at two mosques in Christchurch in 2019 has been prevented from appealing against his guilty pleas, after one of New Zealand’s highest courts said his bid was “utterly devoid of merit”. Brenton Tarrant, who is responsible for the worst mass shooting in New Zealand’s history, asked the court of appeal in February to allow him to a

stories.title: I took an algorithm to court in Sweden. The algorithm won | Charlotta Kronblad
stories.summary: Gothenburg promised to optimise school admissions with a piece of code. The resulting chaos showed how unaccountable systems are ruining lives We like to imagine that injustice announces itself loudly. That when something goes wrong in the public system, alarms go off and someone takes responsibility or is held accountable if they do not. But in 2020 in Gothenburg, injustice arrived quietly, disguised as efficiency. For the first time, the city used an algorithm to allocate places in its schools

stories.title: Jailed Hong Kong pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai wins free speech award in Germany
stories.summary: Media tycoon honoured in absentia as critics decry his 20-year sentence under national security law The jailed media entrepreneur Jimmy Lai has been awarded Deutsche Welle’s freedom of speech award for his contribution to Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement. Jitu Munda, 52, from the Indian state of Odisha, was captured on video carrying the remains of his recently deceased sister through the streets of Keonjhar and placing them outside the local bank. stories.title: Israel intercepts and detains crews of Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
stories.summary: Global Sumud Flotilla describes interception as ‘violent raid’ while Turkey condemns it as ‘act of piracy’ Israeli forces have intercepted and detained the crews of at least 22 boats near the Greek island of Crete from a flotilla that is attempting to break Israel’s maritime blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver humanitarian aid. The Global Sumud Flotilla, consisting of about 58 vessels carrying people from across 70 countries, departed from Italy on Sunday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Georg Baselitz – a life in pictures
stories.summary: The German painter, who has died aged 88 , captured the great rupture of the 20th century, which shaped a body of work that helped redefine postwar art Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘A new chapter’: first commercial flight from US since 2019 lands in Venezuela
stories.summary: Four months after US capture of Nicolás Maduro, officials hail repairing of ties as airliner touches down in Caracas US and Venezuelan officials have hailed a new era in diplomatic relations as the first direct commercial flight between the two countries in more than seven years landed in Caracas. Nearly four months ago, US special forces attack helicopters and planes swept into the skies over Venezuela’s capital after Donald Trump ordered the capture of its president, Nicolás Maduro . Continue

stories.title: Egy képviselőtársának akart beszólni a dörgicsei polgármester, amikor fogyatékkal élőknek az önkormányzat üléseit ajánlotta programként egy dokumentumban
stories.summary: Pflanzner Sándor saját kezűleg írta bele a szövegbe, hogy az ülések lényegében „Maunika sók”, amik „a trash reality kedvelőnek elégíti ki a lakosság egy csekély szeletének összeesküvés-elméletekkel kapcsolatos igényeit”.

stories.title: Szécsi Zoltán mégsem ül be a parlamentbe
stories.summary: Meggondolta magát, nem csatlakozik a Fidesz-frakcióhoz a háromszoros olimpiai bajnok vízilabdázó.

stories.title: A Mistral Európa utolsó esélye
stories.summary: 250 éve egy kis angol startup, a Boulton & Watt kulcsszerepet játszott abban, hogy Európa a világ technológiai központjává vált. Most a francia Mistral AI az utolsó esély arra, hogy ne szoruljon végleg a perifériára.

stories.title: Ezekért érdemes moziba menni május 1-től május 8-ig
stories.summary: Itt a következő hét napra szóló Telex Diszpó.

stories.title: Leépítés kezdődött a Megafonnál, bizonytalan a Fidesz legfontosabb szócsövének jövője
stories.summary: Forrásaink szerint egyelőre egy hónapra elég pénze van a szervezetnek.

stories.title: A dietetika legnagyobb kérdései: jó-e a szénhidrátmentes diéta?
stories.summary: Sorozatunkban a legvitatottabb táplálkozási kérdéseket tesszük fel 5 dietetikusnak. Az első kérdésünk: újra és újra népszerűvé válnak a szénhidrátmegvonásos diéták, amikkel fogyni lehet, de egészségesek vajon?

stories.title: Valószínűleg – válaszolta Trump, amikor megkérdezték, kivonja-e az amerikai csapatokat Olaszországból és Spanyolországból
stories.summary: Egy nappal azután kérdezték erről, hogy amerikai katonákat vonna ki Németországból.

stories.title: Ingyenes lesz a YouTube egyik legjobb funkciója, a kép-a-képben mód
stories.summary: De azért egy kis korlátozást alkalmaznak majd az ingyenes felhasználók esetében.

stories.title: Hirtelen kiadta a 444-nek a kínai gigahitel részleteit az állami adósságkezelő
stories.summary: Ebből kiderül, mennyibe is került a két éve titokban intézett ügylet.

stories.title: What are the sticking points for the EU's new budget?
stories.summary: The battle over the next long-term EU budget has begun. The EU's institutions have staked out their positions and are seeking significantly more funding, but it is the 27 member states that will ultimately decide.

stories.title: King Charles commemorates 9/11 victims in New York visit
stories.summary: The royal couple laid a bouquet at the September 11 Memorial and met with victim's families.

stories.title: Trump threatens US troop cuts in Germany
stories.summary: Donald Trump says the US is considering a "possible reduction" of its forces in Germany just days after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the US was being "humiliated" by Iran's leadership.

stories.title: Whale 'Timmy' reaches Denmark in rescue effort — in pictures
stories.summary: A barge carrying a humpback whale stranded in Germany for weeks has reached Danish waters on its journey to the North Sea.

stories.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

stories.title: Will EU bring in a windfall tax on oil companies?
stories.summary: Five countries, including Germany, want the EU to bring in a so-called windfall tax to target energy companies benefiting from the price crisis. It can be done, but it's complex.

stories.title: First dots on the road map to exiting fossil fuels
stories.summary: The message from inaugural talks on exiting fossil fuels was clear. It's not if, but when and how.

stories.title: How widespread is global fatigue with the US?
stories.summary: The global image of the US has taken a blow, as many people are increasingly fed up with the constant stream of controversies surrounding the Trump administration.

stories.title: Study busts myth: German 'spring fatigue' doesn't exist
stories.summary: Do we really feel more tired in spring? Researchers in Switzerland say, "there's no such thing as spring fatigue." But it is a deep cultural phenomenon in German-speaking countries.

stories.title: Iran's Vafaei playing for more than snooker glory
stories.summary: After years lost to visa battles, Hossein Vafaei is finally delivering on his early promise as a snooker prodigy. He's hoping to inspire people back home amid the turmoil in Iran.

stories.title: Kosovo to hold snap elections in June
stories.summary: Kosovo will soon hold its third parliamentary election in a year and a half. Prime Minister Albin Kurti was able to form a government in February, but now there's a political deadlock over who shoud be elected president.

stories.title: Video shows suspect approaching police after stabbing in north London
stories.summary: Images shared on social media show police tasering and arresting a man with a knife in Golders Green in north London, after two Jewish men were stabbed in an attack. Police declared it a "terrorist incident" and said a 45-year-old man had been arrested, adding that he had also tried to stab officers

stories.title: Three takeaways from Hegseth's clash with lawmakers over Iran war
stories.summary: The hours-long hearing was the first time the US defence secretary had faced questions under oath since the conflict began.

stories.title: A son overlooked and a jailed tycoon: Inside Samsung's succession drama
stories.summary: The family dynasty behind Samsung is so complicated it regularly makes headline news in South Korea.

stories.title: The sub-Arctic town pitching itself as Canada's gateway to Europe
stories.summary: Churchill, Manitoba, best known as the world's polar bear capital, has ambitions of making its deep-water port a year-round trade route.

stories.title: Indians lost $2.5bn to digital fraud in 2025 - now its central bank is fighting back
stories.summary: The RBI is mulling measures to tackle rising cases of digital fraud in India - but will they work?

stories.title: 'Attacked 28 times in a day' - BBC visits heavily targeted US-UK base in Iraq
stories.summary: Before the fragile ceasefire in the region, this was one of the most heavily targeted US and UK military bases in the Middle East.

stories.title: Joy as record-breaking runner Sawe returns home
stories.summary: BBC reports from Kenya, home to Sabastian Sawe, the first man to run a marathon in under two hours.

stories.title: Trump says he is weighing reducing US troop presence in Germany after Iran feud with Merz
stories.summary: Trump's threat comes after Merz earlier this week said that Washington was being “humiliated” by the Iranian leadership, criticising the US lack of strategy in the war.

stories.title: US lawmakers clash with Hegseth on Iran war and $25 billion price tag
stories.summary: The war has cost $25 billion so far (€21 billion), according to Pentagon figures presented to the House Armed Services Committee.

stories.title: Trash piles up in Bali as landfill ban triggers waste crisis
stories.summary: Bali’s landfill ban leads to waste piling up in streets, burning trash, and health concerns as Indonesia moves to enforce long-delayed rules.

stories.title: Oil temporarily hits $126 wartime high as US–Iran standoff fuels supply fears
stories.summary: Oil markets face upward pressure on Thursday, with Brent crude briefly touching $126 as markets price in tensions in the negotiations between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Nairobi welcomes marathon star Sabastian Sawe after record-breaking London victory
stories.summary: Sabastian Sawe returns to Nairobi after breaking the marathon world record in London, becoming the first runner under two hours in a sanctioned race.

stories.title: Germany’s canal village keeps 129-year mail-by-boat tradition alive
stories.summary: In Germany’s Spreewald, postal worker Andrea Bunar resumes delivering mail by barge, continuing a 129-year tradition in Lehde village.

stories.title: Why is China banning drone sales in Beijing?
stories.summary: The BBC's Laura Bicker looks into why China has tightened regulations on drones, with authorities citing public safety concerns.

stories.title: Trump tightens US naval blockade as Iranian rial collapses to record low
stories.summary: The latest drop follows a currency shock several months ago, when the rial fell from around 1.4 million to 1.6 million per US dollar in under a week, as the war moves turns into an economic clash to exhaust the Iranian regime' options.

stories.title: Massive sea lion makes rare appearance in San Francisco
stories.summary: Steller sea lions are more commonly found in the colder waters of Alaska and the Pacific Northwest, making sightings in the Bay Area less common.
